summary: Credential and wallet stealer with hardcoded C2 endpoint
details: Package ships scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js, and build-verifier.js which together implement a host-information and wallet-credential exfiltration tool. scanner.js contains a hardcoded C2 URL at https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 161, 323) and POSTs system identity data (os.hostname(), username via execSync('whoami'), cwd) to that endpoint via https.request. wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem enumeration combined with outbound POST/curl/ping primitives consistent with browser-extension wallet and credential harvesting. build-verifier.js executes 'docker push' via execSync and adds additional https POST channels. The package's name ("deployment-key-auditor") is unrelated to wallet scanning or C2 callbacks; the shipped functionality contradicts the advertised purpose. The combination of hardcoded attacker-controlled C2 (a github.io page bound to https.request POST), system-identity collection (hostname/username/cwd), wallet-targeted filesystem walking, and shell command execution constitutes an unambiguous credential/wallet stealer that fires when the package's modules are loaded or invoked.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
